relationship_description: Many context rows belong to one blueprint.
notes: The core Tier 1 → blueprint relationship. Cascade delete: deleting a blueprint removes its context rows.

relationship_description: Each context row has exactly one content format.
notes: FK to the content_format lookup table. content_format.code is the primary key. Drives rendering and extraction behavior.

relationship_description: Lineage self-reference: a derived context row points to the source context row it was produced from.
notes: NULL for primary context rows. Used for transcripts (derived from audio), OCR (derived from image), AI summaries (derived from raw context), manual extractions, and translations.

relationship_description: Many summary sections belong to one blueprint (zero to eight per blueprint, one per section name).
notes: Tier 2 → blueprint. Cascade delete. A unique constraint on (blueprint_id, section_name) should be considered.

relationship_description: Many model definitions belong to one blueprint.
notes: Tier 3 → blueprint. Cascade delete. A blueprint is the plan; a model definition is a tech-specific realization of it.

relationship_description: Many scenarios belong to one blueprint.
notes: Cascade delete. Scenarios are first-class but always scoped to a blueprint.

relationship_description: Join row points to one blueprint.
notes: Part of M:N between blueprint and domain.

relationship_description: Join row points to one domain.
notes: Part of M:N between blueprint and domain.

relationship_description: Hierarchical self-reference for nested domains.
notes: NULL for top-level domains. See 050_domain_taxonomy.md for the hierarchy.

relationship_description: Join row points to one model definition.
notes: Part of M:N between model_definition and scenario.

relationship_description: Join row points to one scenario.
notes: Part of M:N between model_definition and scenario.

relationship_description: Source side of the blueprint-to-blueprint relationship.
notes: Self-reference via the related_blueprint join table.

relationship_description: Target side of the blueprint-to-blueprint relationship.
notes: Self-reference via the related_blueprint join table.

relationship_description: Join row points to one blueprint.
notes: Part of M:N between blueprint and modeling_feature.

relationship_description: Join row points to one modeling feature.
notes: Part of M:N between blueprint and modeling_feature.

relationship_description: Model definition targets one DES product.
notes: FK from model_definition to des_product lookup. Renamed from target_tech_name (free-text) in schema change #11. Nullable for proposed variants with no committed target tech.

relationship_description: Join row points to one DES product.
notes: Part of M:N between des_product and product_capability. Added in schema change #12.

relationship_description: Join row points to one product capability.
notes: Part of M:N between des_product and product_capability. Added in schema change #12.

relationship_description: Reviewer of the proposal.
notes: FK deferred to future user table. NULL until proposal is reviewed.

relationship_description: Polymorphic reference to the row being proposed. Not a true foreign key because target_table varies.
notes: Polymorphic FK pattern — the combination (target_table, target_row_id) identifies the specific row. Enforcement must happen in application layer, not via a single DB-level FK. When target_table='modeling_feature', target_row_id refers to modeling_feature.code; when 'domain', to domain.domain_id or domain.slug; when 'content_format', to content_format.code.
